data.title: Johnson Outdoors GAAP EPS of -$3.35 misses by $2.37, revenue of $105.87M misses by $9.12M
data.description:
• Johnson Outdoors press release (NASDAQ:JOUT): Q4 GAAP EPS of -$3.35 misses by $2.37.
  • Revenue of $105.87M (+9.9% Y/Y) misses by $9.12M.

data.title: Commodity Roundup: Cocoa surges back to $10,000 a tonne on supply concerns
data.description:
Cocoa futures (CC1:COM) surged above $10,000 per tonne on Tuesday, extending its gains to a fifth straight session, reaching their highest since June 12, supported by deteriorating prospects for supply in the key producing region of West Africa.

“The outlook for the mid-crops have deteriorated in the past weeks,” Steve Wateridge, head of research at TRS by Expana told Bloomberg. “The weather conditions in next three months will determine whether we see further deterioration.”

The key growing regions in West Africa have entered the seasonal Harmattan period that can dry out the soil and stress crops. Lower soil moisture and minimal rains will not support mid-year crop growth, according to weather forecaster Maxar Technologies Inc.

ETFs: (WEAT), (CORN), (SOYB), (DBA), (MOO)

ICE-monitored cocoa inventories held in U.S. ports have reportedly been trending lower for the past 1-1/2 years and fell to a 20-year low last Friday of 1,496,277 bags.

Further supporting the latest leg higher in cocoa prices was the International Cocoa Association upping its 2023/24 global cocoa deficit estimate to -478,000 MT from May's -462,000 MT, the largest deficit in over 60 years.

"World production of cocoa beans for the 2023/24 season ended with a significant reduction as unconducive weather conditions, diseases and pests took a toll on the major producing countries in West Africa," the ICCO said.

Oil futures (CO1:COM) (CL1:COM) meanwhile eased on Tuesday as investors assessed China's promise to step up policy stimulus, while concerns about the collapse of the Syrian government subsided.

ETFs: (USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

Elsewhere on the data front, the U.S. inflation figures are due on Wednesday ahead of a central bank meeting in Canada and rate decisions are due on Thursday from the European Central Bank and the Swiss National Bank.

data.title: Stock index futures almost flat with key inflation data right around the corner
data.description:
Stock index futures were almost flat on Tuesday, a day after Wall Street ended lower, while investors awaited the retail inflation data set to land on Wednesday.

S&P 500 futures (SPX), Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) were unchanged, and Dow futures (INDU) -0.1%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.22%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.14%.

Major U.S. stock averages fell Monday, with market behemoth Nvidia (NVDA) emerging as a source of downward pressure after large-cap stocks marked record highs and gains for the previous week.

"Sectorally, the downside for the S&P 500 index was led by financials and communication services. And matters weren’t helped by Nvidia, which lost ground after China’s State Administration of Market Regulation had opened an antitrust probe," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

Traders are now awaiting the November consumer price index report scheduled to be released on Wedneday. Core CPI is expected to come in at 0.3% M/M, and 3.3% Y/Y.

The CPI is forecasted to come in at 0.2% on a monthly basis, and 2.7% annually.

"That release (CPI) is going to be crucial one, as it’s probably the last big piece of the jigsaw ahead of the Fed’s policy decision next week," Reid said.

On the economic calendar for today, the Q3 productivity and costs report is expected before the opening bell. The nonfarm productivity is anticipated to come in at 2.2% Q/Q, while the unit labor costs at 1.9% Q/Q.

"U.S. productivity data is due—this is everything economists do not understand about the economy, carefully branded to avoid making economists look bad," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

data.title: U.S. finalizes $6.1B+ Chips Act funding with Micron
data.description:
The U.S. Department of Commerce has finalized one of its largest awards to date with memory chipmaker Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU).

The Boise, Idaho-based company had announced a preliminary memorandum of terms with the Department in April for up to ~$6.14B in direct funding under the CHIPS and Science Act.

The $6.1B+ investment in Clay, New York and Boise, Idaho supports the construction of several state-of-the-art memory chip facilities as part of Micron’s total $125B investment over the next few decades, the White House said in a statement on Tuesday.

Additionally, the Department of Commerce has agreed to preliminary terms with Micron (MU) for an additional $275M investment to expand its Manassas, Virginia facility and onshore a critical technology relied upon by the country’s defense industry, automotive sector, and national security community.

The CHIPS Act provides for $39B in grants, billions more in loans and 25% tax credits to boost domestic chip manufacturing and reduce reliance on Asia.

The commerce department has announced preliminary deals with more than 20 companies and has finalized agreements with some companies, including Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM), Intel (INTC), GlobalFoundries (GFS) and Entegris (ENTG). The Biden administration is racing to get the deals finalized before President-elect Donald Trump returns to the White House.

data.title: TeamViewer to acquire Carlyle-backed 1E in $720M deal
data.description:
Remote-working software maker TeamViewer (OTCPK:TMVWF) (OTCPK:TMVWY) is acquiring London-based 1E at an enterprise value of $720M from The Carlyle Group (CG).

TeamViewer's stock fell about 11% on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange on Tuesday.

The Germany-based company said 1E is a leader in Digital Employee Experience, or DEX, management tools. The transaction positions TeamViewer as a strong player in the digital workplace market by integrating TeamViewer's remote access and support expertise with 1E's autonomous IT platform, the company added.

The combined offering would proactively prevent IT issues and provide efficient remote expert support to resolve them, according to the company.

TeamViewer signed an agreement with Carlyle Europe Technology Partners, or CETP, part of The Carlyle Group.

The deal is expected to close in early 2025 after receiving necessary regulatory approvals.

The 1E team, led by CEO Mark Banfield, has on average delivered double-digit profitable revenue growth over the past three years, with annual recurring revenue of $77M (as of September 2024) and more than 99% of the sales have come from Enterprise customers, TeamViewer noted.

Banfield will become a member of TeamViewer's management board, and act as chief commercial officer of the group. In addition, TeamViewer plans to appoint 1E’s Chief Marketing Officer Stephen Tarleton in the same role at TeamViewer.

TeamViewer's Supervisory Board has agreed to a three-year contract extension for chief product and technology officer Mei Dent to continue on the company's product and R&D strategy within the management board.

The company will buy 1E at an enterprise value of $720M on a cash-free, debt-free basis and the financing will be provided via existing credit lines and new debt instruments.

TeamViewer said it is targeting to reduce net leverage ratio to below 2.0x by the end of FY 2026.

data.title: Starbucks China names first chief growth officer amid stiff competition - report
data.description:
U.S. coffee giant Starbucks (NASDAQ:SBUX) appointed its first chief growth officer for China operations amid declining sales in its second-largest market after the U.S., Reuters reported, citing Chinese media outlet.

Tony Yang, formerly a partner and president at the digital marketing agency Tezign, will take on this newly created role, the report said.

As the chief growth officer of Starbucks (SBUX) China, Tony Yang will have several key responsibilities aimed at driving growth and enhancing the company's competitive edge in a challenging market.

The move comes amid restructuring; Molly Liu was named the sole CEO of Starbucks (SBUX) China a few months back, while Belinda Wong transitioned to the role of chairwoman after previously sharing CEO responsibilities.

The company faced declining sales, reporting a 14% drop in comparable store sales for the fiscal year ending September 2024, with both average prices and transaction volumes decreasing significantly.

Starbucks (SBUX) operates over 7,500 stores in China and faces intense competition from local brands such as Luckin Coffee (OTCPK:LKNCY) and Cotti Coffee.

data.title: Arthur J. Gallagher prices $8.5B stock offering
data.description:
• Arthur J. Gallagher (NYSE:AJG) has priced an offering of $8.5B of shares of its common stock at $280.00 per share.
  • Gallagher also granted the underwriters in the offering a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 4,553,571 shares at the same price.
  • Net proceeds of this offering will be used to fund a portion of the cash consideration payable in connection with the company’s proposed acquisition of AssuredPartners and any remaining proceeds for general corporate purposes.
  • In addition to the net proceeds from this offering, Gallagher expects to use available cash and available borrowings under its revolving credit facility or other borrowings to fund the purchase price for the transaction.

data.title: Kingfisher Metals looks to raise C$1.25M via private placement
data.description:
• Kingfisher Metals (OTCQB:KGFMF) said it is looking to raise C$1.25M through a non-brokered private placement of units at C$0.165 apiece.
  • Each unit will have one share and one half of one transferable common share purchase warrant.
  • The firm added each whole warrant will be exercisable to acquire one additional common share of firm for 24 months from the closing date of the offering at an exercise price of C$0.30.
  • The net proceeds are expected to be used for continuing to expand Kingfisher's business, including the acquisition of the Ball Creek West project from P2 Gold, and general working capital purposes.

data.title: Nokia extends partnership with Iliad to enhance 4G and 5G networks in France and Italy
data.description:
• Nokia (NYSE:NOK) and French telecom giant Iliad Group have decided to expand their collaboration that began in 2010 via a new multi-year contract extension to support the operator’s 3G, 4G, and 5G mobile network services across France and Italy.
  • The deal not only encompasses their operations in France and Italy but also extends to the strategic regions of the French Caribbean and the Indian Ocean Islands.
  • Through this multi-year contract extension, Nokia will provide solutions from its AirScale portfolio, including baseband, Massive MIMO radios, Remote Radio Head products, and Core solutions, enabling Iliad to deploy new network services securely in multi-cloud environments.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Tuesday: ORCL, AI, and more
data.description:
Stock futures dipped in Tuesday's premarket session as investors awaited the U.S. consumer price index report, scheduled for release on Wednesday. This report could play a key role in shaping the Federal Reserve's decision on interest rates during its December 17-18 meeting.

Here are some of Tuesday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • C3.ai (AI) shares gained over 7% after the company raised its full-year guidance following stronger-than-expected FQ2 results, driven by robust artificial intelligence-related spending. The company now expects FY2025 revenue to range from $378M to $398M, up from the prior forecast of $370M to $395M, surpassing the consensus of $382.6M. For Q3, C3.ai anticipates revenue between $95.5M and $100.5M, with the midpoint of $98M slightly exceeding the consensus of $97.5M.
  • Vail Resorts (MTN) shares rose nearly 5% after surpassing consensus expectations in its FQ1 results and raising its FY2025 outlook. The company increased its net income guidance for the fiscal year ending July 31, 2025, to a range of $240M to $316M, up from the prior forecast of $224M to $300M, largely driven by the sale of real estate. This revised guidance assumes stable economic conditions, normal weather for the 2024/2025 North American and European ski seasons, the 2025 Australian ski season, and consistent foreign exchange rates.

Biggest stock losers

  • Oracle (ORCL) shares slumped more than 8% after reporting lower-than-expected Q2 results. For the current quarter, the IT giant forecasts revenue growth of 7% to 9%, below the consensus estimate of 10.33%, and expects adjusted earnings of $1.50 to $1.54 per share, matching the consensus of $1.50.
